subject: Physics
solution:
Given quantity is Q=a4b3c2.

The relative error in measuring Q is given by

ΔQQ=4Δaa+3Δbb+2Δcc   ...1

Hence, the required percentage error is given by

ΔQQ×100=4Δaa×100+3Δbb×100+2Δcc×100=4×3%+3×4%+2×5%=12%+12%+10%=34%

subject: Physics
solution:
Given here, 

Length, L=1 m and extension in length,  ΔL=0.4×10-3 m

Mass of load, m=1 kg and diameter of wire, d=0.4×10-3 m

Young's modulus is given by

Y=FLAΔL=mg×1πd240.4×10-3

⇒Y=10×4π0.4×10-32×0.4×10-3

⇒Y=40×722×64×10-3×10-9   

⇒Y=0.199×10-12 N m-2

Error in measurement of Young's modulus,

ΔYY=ΔFF+ΔLL+ΔAA+ΔΔLΔL

=0.020.4+2Δdd=0.24+2×0.010.4

=0.12+0.12=0.1

=0.199×1011≈2×1010

Hence, the value of x=2.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> is not correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is correct.

Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> are correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is not the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>.</mo></math>

subject: Physics
solution:
We know,

∵  OA→+OB→+OC→+OD→+OE→+OF→+OG→+OH→=0→

By triangle law of vector addition, we can write

AB→=AO→+OB→;AC→=AO→+OC→

AD→=AO→+OD→;AE→=AO→+OE→

AF→=AO→+OF→  ;AG→=AO→+OG→

AH→=AO→+OH→

Now

AB→+AC→+AD→+AE→+AF→+AG→+AH→

=7AO→+OB→+OC→+OD→+OE→+OF→+OG→+OH→

=7AO→+0→-OA→

=7AO→+AO→

=8AO→=82i^+3j^-4k^

=16i^+24j^-32k^

subject: Physics
solution:
Given: 

50 VSD=49MSD⇒1VSD=4950MSD

So least count will be

=1MSD-1VSD=120 cm-4950·120 cm

=150×120 cm=0.01 mm

As we know, μ=hh'=5.255.00

Applying log on both sides, we get

lnμ=lnh–lnh’

Differentiating both sides, we get

dμμ=dhh-dh'h'

For calculation, all errors will be added, therefore

dμ=0.015.25+0.015.00μ

=4110×10-3

Hence, x=41

solution:
$\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{P}^1 \mathrm{q}^2 \mathrm{r}^{-3} \mathrm{~s}^{1 / 2}$
$\left(\frac{\mathrm{dC}}{\mathrm{C}}\right)_{\max }=\frac{\mathrm{dP}}{\mathrm{P}}+\frac{2 \mathrm{dq}}{\mathrm{q}}+\frac{3 \mathrm{dr}}{\mathrm{r}}+\frac{1}{2} \frac{\mathrm{ds}}{\mathrm{s}}$
$\begin{aligned}& =\left(1+2 \times 2+3 \times 3+\frac{1}{2} \times 2\right) \% \\& =15 \%\end{aligned}$
Ans. $15$

subject: Physics
solution:
Given: Z=A2B3C4

When quantities are multiplied or divided, the relative error in the result is the sum of the relative errors in the multipliers.

So, ∆ZZ=∆AA+∆AA+∆BB+∆BB+∆BB+∆CC+∆CC+∆CC+∆CC

Thus, relative error is 

∆ZZ=2×∆AA+3×∆BB+4×∆CC

subject: Physics
solution:
Use $\tan \alpha=\frac{P \sin \theta}{Q+P \cos \theta} \Rightarrow \tan 90^{\circ}=\frac{P \sin \theta}{Q+P \cos \theta}=\infty$
$\therefore Q+P \cos \theta=0 \Rightarrow P \cos \theta=-Q$
$\mathrm{R}=\sqrt{\mathrm{P}^2+\mathrm{Q}^2+2 \mathrm{PQ} \cos \theta} \mathrm{R}=\sqrt{\mathrm{P}^2+\mathrm{Q}^2-2 \mathrm{Q}^2}$ or $\mathrm{R}=\sqrt{\mathrm{P}^2-\mathrm{Q}^2}=12$
$144=(\mathrm{P}+\mathrm{Q})(\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{Q})$ or $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{Q}=144 / 18=8 \quad \therefore \mathrm{P}=13 \mathrm{~N}$ and $\mathrm{Q}=5 \mathrm{~N}$

subject: Physics
solution:
The formula to calculate the density of the material of the wire is given by

ρ=MπR2L   ...1

Hence, the formula to calculate the percentage error in calculating the density of the material can be written as

dρρ×100=dMM+2dRR+dLL×100   ...2

Substitute the values of the known parameters into equation (2) to calculate the required percentage error.

=0.010.4+2×0.036+0.048×100=4%

By the given condition,

 B→-(2i^+3j^+2k^)=2j^

 ⇒B→=2i^+5j^+2k^ 

The magnitude is given by the formula,B→=B2x+B2y+B2z
 ⇒|B→|=4+25+4=33

subject: Physics
solution:
$\mathrm{R}=\frac{\mathrm{V}}{\mathrm{i}}$
$\Rightarrow \quad\left|\frac{\Delta \mathrm{R}}{\mathrm{R}}\right|=\left|\frac{\Delta \mathrm{V}}{\mathrm{V}}\right|+\left|\frac{\Delta \mathrm{i}}{\mathrm{i}}\right|$
$\frac{\Delta \mathrm{V}}{\mathrm{V}} \times 100=3$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{\Delta \mathrm{V}}{\mathrm{V}}=0.03$
Similarly, $\frac{\Delta i}{i}=0.03$
Hence $\frac{\Delta \mathrm{R}}{\mathrm{R}}=0.06$
So percentage error is $\frac{\Delta \mathrm{R}}{\mathrm{R}} \times 100=6 \%$

subject: Physics
solution:
For two vectors to be perpendicular, their dot product is equal to zero.

So, P→·Q→=0

⇒i^+2mj^+mk^·4i^-2j^+mk^=0⇒4-4m+m2=0⇒m-22=0⇒m=2
